disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Injury occurred while sanding wood without safety goggles — high-velocity particulate mechanism
trigger_question_examples: What were you doing when this started?
Were you wearing any eye protection?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I was sanding some wood and didn't have safety goggles on.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden reduction in visual acuity in the affected eye
trigger_question_examples: Has your vision changed in that eye?
Can you see as clearly as usual out of that eye?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, everything is blurry in that eye, much worse than normal.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Extreme photophobia requiring the room lights to be off
trigger_question_examples: Is light making the pain worse?
Do you need to keep the room dark?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, any light at all makes it so much worse, I've had to keep the lights off.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No visible foreign body or irregular pupil noticed by patient/roommate on gentle inspection
trigger_question_examples: Has your roommate looked closely at your eye — does the pupil look round and normal, or an odd shape?
Can you see anything stuck in your eye?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My roommate looked and didn't see anything obviously stuck in it, and said the pupil still looks round, not weirdly shaped.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Can I just put some numbing eye drops in and go to bed, and see a doctor tomorrow if it's still bad?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should advise against self-administered topical anaesthetic (not available over the counter and unsafe for repeated/unsupervised use, can mask worsening injury and delay healing), and should direct urgent same-day assessment rather than waiting until tomorrow given the risk of infectious keratitis or missed penetrating injury.
